The BRQS Module (JNN)

A Four Behavioral State Model with Infection Dynamics

David L Smith, Doreen Mbabazi Ssebuliba, Juliet Nakakawa Nsumba

March 25, 2026

Source: vignettes/MYZ-BRQS_JNN.Rmd
MYZ-BRQS_JNN.Rmd
MYname = "BRQS"

For the MY component, the BRQS module implements a behavioral state model for mosquito ecology and infection dynamics with two orgthogonal state spaces. It is a system of ordinary differential equations. The following concise summary is explained in greater length below:

  • The behavioral states are:

    • blood feeding (BB);

    • post-prandial resting (RR);

    • egg laying (QQ); and

    • sugar feeding (SS)

  • The infection states are

    • uninfected (uu);

    • infected (yy); and

    • infectious (zz);

  • The full model thus has 12 variables per patch:

    • 3 variables describe blood feeding mosquitoes: Bu,B_u,By,B_y, and BzB_z;

    • 3 variables describe resting mosquitoes: RuR_u, Ry,R_y, and RzR_z;

    • 3 variables describe egg laying mosquitoes: Qu,Q_u,Qy,Q_y, and QzQ_z;

    • 3 variables describe sugar feeding mosquitoes: SuS_u, SyS_y, and Sz.S_z.

  • Two important details about infection dynamics are that:

    • Mosquitoes move from BuB_u to RyR_y when they blood feed on an infectious human and get infected: in this model, that occurs at the per-capita rate fqκfq\kappa; and

    • Mosquitoes in all infected states (yy) move to infectious states (zz) at the rate ϕ,\phi, where ϕ−1\phi^{-1} is the EIP.

This vignette describes the model and its implementation in ramp.library.

Figure 1: Flow Diagram for mosquito ecology part of the BRQS Model for surviving mosquitoes. The figure does not include arrows for mosquito mortality or emigration-related loss from the system.

Basic Dynamics

Behavioral State Dynamics: A Compartment Model

In the basic compartmental model, there are four behavioral states:

  • BB the density of blood feeding mosquitoes;

  • RR the density of resting mosquitoes;

  • QQ the density of egg laying mosquitoes.

  • SS the density of sugar feeding mosquitoes;

Mosquitoes emerge from aquatic habitat at the patch-specific rate Λ,\Lambda, and …

Behavioral Parameters

  • ff is the overall blood feeding rate;

  • ν\nu is the egg laying rate;

  • δ\delta is the fraction of mosquitoes that transition into a sugar feeding state after laying eggs; the remaining δ̂=1−δ\hat \delta = 1-\delta transition into a blood feeding state. In some models, we let δ\delta be a function of the local availability of sugar.

  • ξ\xi is rate that blood feeding mosquitoes switch to sugar feeding

  • θ\theta is rate gravid mosquitoes resorb their eggs, reverting to a blood feeding state; while we do not explicitly model opportunistic sugar feeding while laying eggs, we assume mosquitoes would remain in the egg laying state if sugar was readily available, so θ\theta could be described using a functional response to available sugar

  • Ωx\Omega_x is a state-specific dispersal matrix:

    • gxg_x is the state-specific mortality rate

    • σx\sigma_x is the state-specific emigration rate,

    • μx\mu_x is state-specific survival associated with

    • KxK_x the state-specific dispersal matrix;

    • The demographic matrix is Ωx=gx+σx(μx−Kx)\Omega_x = g_x + \sigma_x(\mu_x- K_x)

  • ζ\zeta is the refeeding rate

  • η−1\eta^{-1} is the duration of the post-prandial resting period

Behavioral Dynamics

Ḃ=Λ(1−ω)+ρS+(ν+θ)(1−δ)Q+ζR−(f+ξB)B−ΩB⋅BṠ=Λω+(ν+θ)δQ+ξB−ρS−ΩS⋅SṘ=fB−(ζ+η+g)RQ̇=ηR−(ν+θ)Q−ΩQ⋅Q \begin{array}{rl} \dot B &= \Lambda (1-\omega) + \rho S + (\nu + \theta) (1-\delta) Q + \zeta R - (f + \xi_B) B - \Omega_B \cdot B \\ \dot S &= \Lambda \omega+ (\nu + \theta)\delta Q + \xi B - \rho S - \Omega_S \cdot S \\ \dot R &= f B - (\zeta + \eta + g) R \\ \dot Q &= \eta R - (\nu + \theta) Q - \Omega_Q \cdot Q \\ \end{array}

In this version, mosquitoes switch back into a blood feeding state after laying eggs. A parameter ξ\xi determines whether the mosquitoes would switch into a sugar feeding state from either a blood feeding or egg laying state.

Note that since all sugar feeding mosquitoes transition into a blood feeding state, the transition into sugar feeding from egg laying implies the eggs have been reabsorbed.

Infection Dynamics

Variables

  • Each one of the behavioral states is replicated for each one of the infection states. The variable names add a subscript uu for uninfected; yy for infected but not yet infectious; and zz for infectious:

  • SuS_u, SyS_y, and SzS_z are the densities of uninfected, infected but not infectious, and infectious sugar feeding mosquitoes, respectively.

  • BuB_u, ByB_y, and BzB_z are the densities of uninfected, infected but not infectious, and infectious blood feeding mosquitoes, respectively.

  • RuR_u, RyR_y, and RzR_z are the densities of uninfected, infected but not infectious, and infectious resting mosquitoes, respectively.

  • QuQ_u, QyQ_y, and QzQ_z are the densities of uninfected, infected but not infectious, and infectious egg laying mosquitoes, respectively.

Terms

κ\kappa is the NI, the probability of becoming infected after blood feeding on a human.

Parameters

  • ϕ−1\phi^{-1} is the EIP

  • qq is the human fraction for blood feeding

We present the following behavioral state model:

uninfected

Ḃu=Λ(1−ω)+ρSu+(ν+θ)(1−δ)Qu+ζR−(f+ξ)Bu−ΩB⋅BuṠu=Λω+ξBu+(ν+θ)δQu−ρSu−ΩS⋅SuṘu=f(1−qκ)Bu−(ζ+η+g)RuQ̇u=ηRu−(ν+θ)Qu−ΩQ⋅Qu \begin{array}{rl} \dot B_u &= \Lambda (1-\omega) + \rho S_u + (\nu +\theta) (1-\delta) Q_u + \zeta R - (f + \xi) B_u - \Omega_B \cdot B_u \\ \dot S_u &= \Lambda \omega + \xi B_u + (\nu + \theta) \delta Q_u - \rho S_u - \Omega_S \cdot S_u \\ \dot R_u &= f (1-q\kappa) B_u - (\zeta + \eta + g) R_u \\ \dot Q_u &= \eta R_u - (\nu + \theta) Q_u- \Omega_Q \cdot Q_u \end{array} infected

Ṡy=ξBy+(ν+θ)δQy−ρSy−ΩS⋅Sy−ϕSyḂy=ρSy+(ν+θ)(1−δ)Qy+ζRy−(f+ξ)By−ΩB⋅By−ϕByṘy=fqκBu+fBy−(ζ+η+g)Ry−ϕRyQ̇y=ηRy−(ν+θ)Qy−ΩQ⋅Qy−ϕQy \begin{array}{rl} \dot S_y &= \xi B_y + (\nu + \theta) \delta Q_y - \rho S_y - \Omega_S \cdot S_y -\phi S_y\\ \dot B_y &= \rho S_y + (\nu +\theta)(1-\delta) Q_y + \zeta R_y - (f + \xi) B_y - \Omega_B \cdot B_y -\phi B_y\\ \dot R_y &= f q \kappa B_u + f B_y- (\zeta + \eta + g) R_y - \phi R_y\\ \dot Q_y &= \eta R_y - (\nu + \theta) Q_y- \Omega_Q \cdot Q_y - \phi Q_y \end{array}

infectious

Ṡz=ϕSy+ξBz+(ν+θ)δQz−ρSz−ΩS⋅SzḂz=ϕBy+(ν+θ)(1−δ)Qz+ρSz+ζRz−(f+ξ)Bz−ΩB⋅BzṘz=ϕRy+fBz−(ζ+η+g)RzQ̇z=ϕQy+ηRz−(ν+θ)Qz−ΩQ⋅Qz \begin{array}{rl} \dot S_z &= \phi S_y + \xi B_z + (\nu+\theta) \delta Q_z - \rho S_z - \Omega_S \cdot S_z \\ \dot B_z &= \phi B_y + (\nu+\theta)(1- \delta) Q_z + \rho S_z + \zeta R_z - (f + \xi) B_z - \Omega_B \cdot B_z \\ \dot R_z &= \phi R_y + f B_z - (\zeta + \eta + g) R_z \\ \dot Q_z &= \phi Q_y + \eta R_z - (\nu + \theta) Q_z- \Omega_Q \cdot Q_z \end{array}

Developed by David L. Smith, Juliet Nsumba Nakakawa, Doreen Mbabazi Ssebuliba.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
